message.content:
You are fixing reviewed bugs in the retrieval / validation / oracle-engines layer of the repo at C:\repos\oracle. Make MINIMAL, conservative edits. Read each file and surrounding code before editing. Do NOT commit. Do NOT touch files outside this list (in particular do NOT edit packages/ai/src/embeddings.ts or any provider adapter — another agent owns those). After editing, run typecheck for the affected packages (`pnpm --filter @oracle/ai typecheck` and `pnpm --filter @oracle/engines typecheck`, or the tsc equivalent if no such script) and report what you ran + results.

PRESERVE these documented-intentional patterns (do NOT "fix" them): raw-SQL list params bound as JSON strings via `IN (SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(...))`; EMBEDDING_DIM inlined via sql.raw; the two retrieval SQL branches (hybrid + tsvector fallback) intentionally both build WHERE from buildPlanMetadataFilters.

FILES + FIXES:

1. packages/oracle-engines/src/extraction/stage-entity-proposal.ts (lines ~54-58) — uses `${args.rawString} = ANY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(raw_strings_observed))`, which is invalid Postgres and diverges from the repo convention. Fix to the endorsed form: `${args.rawString} IN (SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(raw_strings_observed))`. Verify there are no other `= ANY(SELECT ...)` occurrences in this file; fix them the same way if present.

2. packages/ai/src/retrieval-plan.ts — `DOMAIN_KEYWORDS` (lines ~110-373) has no entry for two real top-level domains: `creative_design` and `vendor_management` (both defined in packages/shared/src/domains.ts), so queries about them fall to global_fallback. ADD reasonable keyword groups for both (look at how the existing groups are shaped and at the domain descriptions in domains.ts / AGENTS.md to choose keywords — e.g. creative_design: art, artwork, design concept, illustration, mockup, color, print design, packaging art; vendor_management: vendor management, vendor onboarding, vendor scorecard, vendor audit, supplier evaluation — but note vendor-management-adjacent terms currently sit under supply_chain, so choose terms that disambiguate). Then in `inferTopDomainExclusions` (line ~618-653) the operations-systems branch excludes `creative_design` (a real domain) which silently drops its claims — add a one-line comment explaining the intentional exclusion now that creative_design is inferable, OR if it reads as accidental, remove `creative_design` from that exclusion list. Use your judgment and state which you chose and why in your report. Do NOT change the JSON-string filter mechanics.

3. packages/ai/src/retrieval.ts — the tsvector fallback path sets `distance = 1 - ts_score` (line ~457) which can go negative and is on a non-comparable scale vs the hybrid cosine distance (line ~372). Fix: clamp the fallback to `GREATEST(0, 1 - ts_score)` in the SQL (or Math.max(0, ...) if computed in JS) so it can't be negative, and add a short code comment that `distance` is path-dependent and must not be cross-thresholded. Do not alter the WHERE/filter logic or the hybrid path ranking.

4. packages/ai/src/validation/evidence-validator.ts (lines ~53-70) — the offset-match path checks `slice === exactQuote` but doesn't validate offset sanity. Add guards BEFORE slicing: require both charStart and charEnd are numbers (reject if exactly one is provided), require charStart >= 0, charStart <= charEnd, charEnd <= sourceText.length; if any fails, fall through to / return the non-offset scan path result as appropriate (mirror the stricter checks already in packages/oracle-engines/src/extraction/quote-validator.ts lines ~67-103). Keep the existing scan-path behavior intact.

5. packages/oracle-engines/src/extraction/quote-validator.ts — `validateSourcePointer` switch (lines ~246-286) has no `default`, so an unexpected sourceType returns undefined and violates the return type. Add a `default` branch returning a failed result like `{ ok: false, failedCheckName: 'source_type_valid', detail: \`unknown source type: ${sourceType}\` }` (match the existing SourcePointerValidationResult shape exactly — read the type first).

6. packages/oracle-engines/src/synthesis/diff-validator.ts (lines ~332-335) — entity-support check uses substring match for summaries but exact-equality for the registry canonicals, an asymmetry causing spurious holds (e.g. "Walt Disney Company" vs registry "walt disney"). Make the registry side also use a token/substring-aware match consistent with the summary side (e.g. also accept when a registry canonical is a substring of the candidate phrase or vice-versa). Keep it conservative — this only affects whether synthesis is held for review; do not make it accept fabricated entities wholesale. Add a brief comment. If you judge this too risky to change behaviorally, instead just add a clear comment documenting the asymmetry and leave logic as-is — state your choice in the report.

Report a concise per-file summary + typecheck result.

1	// Write-time fuzzy-dedup for entity_proposals.
2	//
3	// Uses pg_trgm similarity (threshold 0.85) to detect near-duplicate proposals
4	// for the same (entity_type, canonical_value) surface before inserting.
5	//
6	// If a sufficiently similar pending/approved proposal exists:
7	//   → increment proposal_count and append the new rawString to raw_strings_observed.
8	// If not:
9	//   → insert a fresh row.
10	//
11	// This keeps the admin queue clean as the same unknown entity (e.g. "Disney",
12	// "Walt Disney", "Disney approvals") gets extracted repeatedly before review.
13	
14	import { sql } from 'drizzle-orm';
15	import type { OracleDb } from '@oracle/db';
16	
17	export interface StageEntityProposalArgs {
18	  proposedEntityType: string;
19	  proposedCanonicalValue: string;
20	  rawString: string;
21	  observedInSourceType: 'claim_candidate' | 'document_chunk';
22	  observedInSourceId: string;
23	  proposedByModelRunId: string | null;
24	}
25	
26	export async function stageEntityProposal(
27	  db: OracleDb,
28	  args: StageEntityProposalArgs,
29	): Promise<{ action: 'inserted' | 'merged'; proposalId: string }> {
30	  // Similarity threshold: 0.85 — catches obvious surface-form variants while
31	  // avoiding false positives between genuinely different entities.
32	  const SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.85;
33	
34	  type ExistingRow = { id: string };
35	
36	  const existing = await db.execute(sql`
37	    SELECT id FROM entity_proposals
38	    WHERE proposed_entity_type = ${args.proposedEntityType}
39	      AND status IN ('pending', 'approved')
40	      AND similarity(proposed_canonical_value, ${args.proposedCanonicalValue}) >= ${SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD}
41	    ORDER BY similarity(proposed_canonical_value, ${args.proposedCanonicalValue}) DESC
42	    LIMIT 1
43	  `);
44	
45	  const existingRow = ([...existing][0] ?? null) as ExistingRow | null;
46	
47	  if (existingRow) {
48	    // Merge: increment count, append the new raw string if not already present.
49	    await db.execute(sql`
50	      UPDATE entity_proposals
51	      SET
52	        proposal_count        = proposal_count + 1,
53	        raw_strings_observed  = (
54	          CASE
55	            WHEN ${args.rawString} = ANY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(raw_strings_observed))
56	            THEN raw_strings_observed
57	            ELSE raw_strings_observed || to_jsonb(${args.rawString}::text)
58	          END
59	        )
60	      WHERE id = ${existingRow.id}
61	    `);
62	    return { action: 'merged', proposalId: existingRow.id };
63	  }
64	
65	  // Insert fresh proposal.
66	  type InsertedRow = { id: string };
67	  const inserted = await db.execute(sql`
68	    INSERT INTO entity_proposals (
69	      proposed_entity_type,
70	      proposed_canonical_value,
71	      raw_strings_observed,
72	      observed_in_source_type,
73	      observed_in_source_id,
74	      status,
75	      proposed_by_model_run_id,
76	      proposal_count
77	    ) VALUES (
78	      ${args.proposedEntityType},
79	      ${args.proposedCanonicalValue},
80	      ${JSON.stringify([args.rawString])}::jsonb,
81	      ${args.observedInSourceType},
82	      ${args.observedInSourceId}::uuid,
83	      'pending',
84	      ${args.proposedByModelRunId ? sql`${args.proposedByModelRunId}::uuid` : sql`NULL`},
85	      1
86	    )
87	    RETURNING id
88	  `);
89	
90	  const insertedRow = ([...inserted][0] ?? null) as InsertedRow | null;
91	  if (!insertedRow) throw new Error('[stageEntityProposal] INSERT returned no row');
92	
93	  return { action: 'inserted', proposalId: insertedRow.id };
94	}

1	// RetrievalPlan — spec docs/oracle/07-knowledge-segmentation.md §"What this does to retrieval code".
2	//
3	// Every query to the Oracle knowledge graph MUST go through a RetrievalPlan.
4	// Global vector search without metadata pre-filtering is forbidden (spec rule from
5	// 00-buildout-index.md "Retrieval rule" and 07-knowledge-segmentation.md).
6	//
7	// Enforcement mechanism: the `searchScope` field on every RetrievalPlan records
8	// HOW the scope was determined.  searchWithRetrievalPlan() emits a structured
9	// console.warn for every 'global_fallback' plan so operators can audit
10	//   SELECT ... WHERE selected_domains @> ARRAY['_global_fallback']
11	// in the oracle_context_packs table and improve heuristics accordingly.
12	//
13	// This module provides:
14	//   - RetrievalPlanSearchScope — 'domain_filtered' | 'global_fallback' | 'global_explicit'
15	//   - RetrievalPlan type (matches the spec shape exactly + searchScope).
16	//   - buildRetrievalPlanFromQuery() — lightweight synchronous heuristic builder.
17	//   - buildDomainScopedPlan()       — explicit domain list, no heuristic exclusions.
18	//   - buildGlobalRetrievalPlan()    — intentional all-corpus search.
19	//
20	// A model-backed variant (buildRetrievalPlanWithModel) can be added later when
21	// per-query latency budgets allow an extra cheap structured-output call.
22	
23	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	// Types
25	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	
27	/**
28	 * How the retrieval scope was determined.
29	 *
30	 *   domain_filtered  — ≥1 domain matched from keyword heuristics or explicit
31	 *                      caller input. Search is scoped; vendor-manual noise is
32	 *                      suppressed from unrelated results.
33	 *   global_fallback  — 0 domains matched. Falling back to full-corpus search.
34	 *                      searchWithRetrievalPlan() logs a structured warning so
35	 *                      the gap can be closed by adding keywords to DOMAIN_KEYWORDS.
36	 *   global_explicit  — Caller intentionally requested all domains (e.g. the
37	 *                      contradiction-watcher checking a claim with no taxonomy
38	 *                      tags yet, or a broad synthesis sweep).
39	 */
40	export type RetrievalPlanSearchScope =
41	  | 'domain_filtered'
42	  | 'global_fallback'
43	  | 'global_explicit';
44	
45	export type RetrievalPlan = {
46	  /** Up to 3 top-level domain IDs from knowledge_top_domains. Empty = search all. */
47	  topDomainHints: string[];
48	  /** Entity references the query is about; matched against canonical entity registry. */
49	  requiredEntities: { entityType: string; canonicalValue: string }[];
50	  /** Document classes to exclude from the candidate set (e.g. vendor_manual for system questions). */
51	  excludedDocumentClasses?: string[];
52	  /** Entity types whose presence in a claim should exclude it. */
53	  excludedEntityTypes?: string[];
54	  /** Top-level domain IDs to exclude from the search. */
55	  excludedTopDomains?: string[];
56	  /** Process stage hints for narrowing retrieval to a specific lifecycle stage. */
57	  processStageHints?: string[];
58	  /**
59	   * Time validity filter.
60	   *   'current' — only claims whose effective_until IS NULL (still in force).
61	   *   'all'     — no time filter.
62	   *   'since:YYYY-MM-DD' — claims effective after a given date.
63	   */
64	  timeFilter?: 'current' | 'all' | `since:${string}`;
65	  /** The actual semantic query string sent to the embedding + tsvector search. */
66	  vectorQuery: string;
67	  /** Maximum number of results to return. */
68	  topK: number;
69	  /**
70	   * How the retrieval scope was determined.
71	   * Used for observability — searchWithRetrievalPlan() warns on global_fallback.
72	   * Chat route stores this in oracle_context_packs.selected_domains as
73	   * '_global_fallback' so operators can query the fallback rate.
74	   */
75	  searchScope: RetrievalPlanSearchScope;
76	  /**
77	   * Optional pre-computed embedding for `vectorQuery`.
78	   * When present, searchWithRetrievalPlan() skips the embedText() call and uses
79	   * this vector directly, saving one OpenAI API round-trip.
80	   *
81	   * Must be exactly EMBEDDING_DIM (1536) floats.  The canonical use-case is
82	   * contradiction-watcher: it already computes/stores the claim embedding once
83	   * to persist it in `claims.embedding`, then threads the same vector here so
84	   * the subsequent ANN search doesn't embed the same summary a second time.
85	   */
86	  precomputedVector?: number[];
87	  /**
88	   * Department(s) of the employee making the request.
89	   * Used by searchWithRetrievalPlan() to apply a small RRF score bonus to
90	   * claims whose claim_metadata.department matches one of these values.
91	   * This is a soft signal — it nudges ranking but never filters results.
92	   * Sourced from employees.departments (the full array) at query time.
93	   */
94	  departmentHints?: string[];
95	};
96	
97	export const DEFAULT_TOP_K = 8;
98

417	/**
418	 * Build a RetrievalPlan from a natural-language query using lightweight
419	 * keyword heuristics. No model call — synchronous.
420	 *
421	 * Callers may pass explicit overrides (e.g. the Oracle tool caller already
422	 * knows the domains from context); those take priority over heuristic inference.
423	 *
424	 * Sets searchScope:
425	 *   'domain_filtered' — ≥1 domain matched.
426	 *   'global_fallback' — 0 domains matched; searchWithRetrievalPlan() will warn.
427	 *
428	 * @example
429	 * const plan = buildRetrievalPlanFromQuery(
430	 *   "When does an image get uploaded to the ERP system?",
431	 *   { topK: 10 }
432	 * );
433	 * // → topDomainHints: ['it_systems', 'production_lifecycle']
434	 * //   excludedDocumentClasses: ['vendor_manual']
435	 * //   excludedEntityTypes: ['licensor', 'vendor']
436	 * //   searchScope: 'domain_filtered'
437	 */
438	export function buildRetrievalPlanFromQuery(
439	  query: string,
440	  opts?: Partial<
441	    Pick<
442	      RetrievalPlan,
443	      | 'topDomainHints'
444	      | 'requiredEntities'
445	      | 'excludedDocumentClasses'
446	      | 'excludedEntityTypes'
447	      | 'topK'
448	      | 'timeFilter'
449	      | 'processStageHints'
450	      | 'precomputedVector'
451	      | 'departmentHints'
452	      | 'excludedTopDomains'
453	    >
454	  >,
455	): RetrievalPlan {
456	  const q = query.toLowerCase();
457	
458	  const inferredTopDomainHints: string[] =
459	    opts?.topDomainHints && opts.topDomainHints.length > 0
460	      ? opts.topDomainHints.slice(0, 3)
461	      : inferTopDomains(q);
462	
463	  const excludedEntityTypes: string[] =
464	    opts?.excludedEntityTypes && opts.excludedEntityTypes.length > 0
465	      ? opts.excludedEntityTypes
466	      : inferEntityExclusions(q);
467	
468	  const excludedDocumentClasses: string[] =
469	    opts?.excludedDocumentClasses && opts.excludedDocumentClasses.length > 0
470	      ? opts.excludedDocumentClasses
471	      : inferDocumentClassExclusions(q);
472	
473	  const excludedTopDomains: string[] =
474	    opts?.excludedTopDomains && opts.excludedTopDomains.length > 0
475	      ? opts.excludedTopDomains
476	      : inferTopDomainExclusions(q, inferredTopDomainHints);
477	
478	  const topDomainHints =
479	    excludedTopDomains.length > 0
480	      ? inferredTopDomainHints.filter((d) => !excludedTopDomains.includes(d)).slice(0, 3)
481	      : inferredTopDomainHints;
482	
483	  const searchScope: RetrievalPlanSearchScope =
484	    topDomainHints.length > 0 ? 'domain_filtered' : 'global_fallback';
485	
486	  return {
487	    topDomainHints,
488	    requiredEntities: opts?.requiredEntities ?? [],
489	    excludedDocumentClasses: excludedDocumentClasses.length > 0 ? excludedDocumentClasses : undefined,
490	    excludedEntityTypes: excludedEntityTypes.length > 0 ? excludedEntityTypes : undefined,
491	    excludedTopDomains: excludedTopDomains.length > 0 ? excludedTopDomains : undefined,
492	    processStageHints: opts?.processStageHints,
493	    timeFilter: opts?.timeFilter ?? 'current',
494	    vectorQuery: query,
495	    topK: opts?.topK ?? DEFAULT_TOP_K,
496	    searchScope,
497	    precomputedVector: opts?.precomputedVector,
498	    departmentHints: opts?.departmentHints,
499	  };
500	}
501	
502	/**
503	 * Build a RetrievalPlan with explicitly-provided domain hints and NO heuristic
504	 * entity/document-class exclusion inference.
505	 *
506	 * Use this when the caller already knows the exact domains (e.g. contradiction-
507	 * watcher passing the target claim's claim_top_domains). Avoids accidental
508	 * exclusions when the intent is to search broadly within known domains.
509	 *
510	 * Sets searchScope: 'domain_filtered' if domainHints is non-empty,
511	 *                   'global_fallback' if empty (caller should use buildGlobalRetrievalPlan instead).
512	 */
513	export function buildDomainScopedPlan(
514	  query: string,
515	  topDomainHints: string[],
516	  opts?: Partial<
517	    Pick<
518	      RetrievalPlan,
519	      | 'topK'
520	      | 'timeFilter'
521	      | 'requiredEntities'
522	      | 'excludedDocumentClasses'
523	      | 'excludedEntityTypes'
524	      | 'precomputedVector'
525	    >
526	  >,
527	): RetrievalPlan {
528	  return {
529	    topDomainHints: topDomainHints.slice(0, 3),
530	    requiredEntities: opts?.requiredEntities ?? [],
531	    excludedDocumentClasses: opts?.excludedDocumentClasses,
532	    excludedEntityTypes: opts?.excludedEntityTypes,
533	    excludedTopDomains: undefined,
534	    processStageHints: undefined,
535	    timeFilter: opts?.timeFilter ?? 'current',
536	    vectorQuery: query,
537	    topK: opts?.topK ?? DEFAULT_TOP_K,
538	    searchScope: topDomainHints.length > 0 ? 'domain_filtered' : 'global_fallback',
539	    precomputedVector: opts?.precomputedVector,
540	  };
541	}
542	
543	/**
544	 * Build a RetrievalPlan that intentionally searches the full claim corpus.
545	 *
546	 * Use when the caller explicitly wants all domains — e.g. a broad synthesis
547	 * sweep, a re-indexing job, or a diagnostic search. Marks searchScope as
548	 * 'global_explicit' so monitoring can distinguish intentional global searches
549	 * from heuristic failures (global_fallback).
550	 *
551	 * Do NOT use this for ordinary chat-route retrieval. Use buildRetrievalPlanFromQuery
552	 * so the query goes through domain inference and gets a domain_filtered scope.
553	 */
554	export function buildGlobalRetrievalPlan(
555	  query: string,
556	  opts?: Partial<
557	    Pick<
558	      RetrievalPlan,
559	      | 'topK'
560	      | 'timeFilter'
561	      | 'excludedDocumentClasses'
562	      | 'requiredEntities'
563	      | 'precomputedVector'
564	    >
565	  >,
566	): RetrievalPlan {
567	  return {
568	    topDomainHints: [],
569	    requiredEntities: opts?.requiredEntities ?? [],
570	    excludedDocumentClasses: opts?.excludedDocumentClasses,
571	    excludedEntityTypes: undefined,
572	    excludedTopDomains: undefined,
573	    processStageHints: undefined,
574	    timeFilter: opts?.timeFilter ?? 'current',
575	    vectorQuery: query,
576	    topK: opts?.topK ?? DEFAULT_TOP_K,
577	    searchScope: 'global_explicit',
578	    precomputedVector: opts?.precomputedVector,
579	  };
580	}

582	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
583	// Private heuristic helpers
584	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
585	
586	function inferTopDomains(query: string): string[] {
587	  const scores = new Map<string, number>();
588	  for (const { domainId, keywords } of DOMAIN_KEYWORDS) {
589	    const score = keywords.filter((k) => query.includes(k)).length;
590	    if (score > 0) scores.set(domainId, score);
591	  }
592	  return Array.from(scores.entries())
593	    .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
594	    .slice(0, 3)
595	    .map(([id]) => id);
596	}
597	
598	function inferEntityExclusions(query: string): string[] {
599	  const excluded = new Set<string>();
600	  for (const { keywords, excludeEntityTypes } of ENTITY_EXCLUSION_HINTS) {
601	    if (keywords.some((k) => query.includes(k))) {
602	      for (const t of excludeEntityTypes) excluded.add(t);
603	    }
604	  }
605	  return Array.from(excluded);
606	}
607	
608	function inferDocumentClassExclusions(query: string): string[] {
609	  const excluded = new Set<string>();
610	  for (const { keywords, excludeClasses } of DOCUMENT_CLASS_EXCLUSIONS) {
611	    if (keywords.some((k) => query.includes(k))) {
612	      for (const c of excludeClasses) excluded.add(c);
613	    }
614	  }
615	  return Array.from(excluded);
616	}
617	
618	function inferTopDomainExclusions(query: string, topDomainHints: string[]): string[] {
619	  if (topDomainHints.includes('operations_systems')) {
620	    const looksLikeOperationsSystems =
621	      [
622	        'designflow', 'design flow', 'plm', 'google sheets', 'google sheet',
623	        'orderlist', 'order list', 'masterdata', 'master data', 'tasklist',
624	        'task list', 'field mapping', 'map fields', 'data migration',
625	        'spreadsheet to plm', 'sheets to plm', 'source of truth',
626	      ].some((k) => query.includes(k));
627	
628	    if (looksLikeOperationsSystems) {
629	      // Data-flow questions about business systems should not be pulled into
630	      // generic customer orders, licensor approvals, or design-art workflow
631	      // just because the source sheet or PLM name contains overlapping words.
632	      return ['customer_ops', 'licensing_approvals', 'creative_design'];
633	    }
634	  }
635	
636	  if (!topDomainHints.includes('design_file_operations')) return [];
637	
638	  const looksLikeFileOps =
639	    [
640	      'file naming', 'filename', 'file name', 'invalid character', 'save files',
641	      'saving files', 'bloated', 'file size', 'folder structure', 'shared folder',
642	      'server folder', 'design server', 'linked asset', 'embedded asset',
643	      'missing link', 'package links', 'artwork file', 'design files',
644	      'designer files', 'creative files',
645	    ].some((k) => query.includes(k));
646	
647	  if (!looksLikeFileOps) return [];
648	
649	  // File-operations questions are about keeping creative files usable, small,
650	  // compatible, and findable. They should not silently drift into product
651	  // approval/status workflow unless the user asks that workflow question.
652	  return ['product_development', 'production_lifecycle', 'it_systems'];
653	}
